desc: Sidi Ifni is a windswept Atlantic town with Spanish Art Deco remnants, surf access, and a slower pace than Morocco's major resort corridors.
hls: Distinctive Art Deco facades and sea-cliff setting
Nearby surf beaches like Legzira and Mirleft
Strong value compared with Agadir-area hotspots
Mild winters ideal for coastal road trips

todo.name: Seafront Art Deco walk
todo.desc: Explore old Spanish-quarter architecture and cliffside promenade.

todo.name: Legzira arches visit
todo.desc: Time the beach walk around low tide for arch access.

todo.name: Sidi Ifni market morning
todo.desc: Browse weekly souk produce, fish, and household stalls.

todo.name: Mirleft surf session
todo.desc: Book a beginner or intermediate surf lesson nearby.

todo.name: Plage Blanche excursion
todo.desc: Take a guided 4x4 trip to long undeveloped Atlantic sands.

todo.name: Sunset at Place Hassan II
todo.desc: Join evening promenade culture in the central square.

wifi.note: Generally fine in town riads; weaker in outer beach properties.

prac.fltNote: No reliable long-haul service into nearby minor airports, so UK/Ireland visitors usually fly to Agadir or Marrakech then drive south; winter sun season raises demand.
prac.currency: Morocco local currency (check live rates before booking).
prac.lang: Local language plus tourism-level English in main visitor areas.
prac.tz: Check local timezone and DST rules for exact travel dates.
prac.visa: Confirm current entry/transit rules for your passport before booking.
alerts: Atlantic swell and rip currents are strong on exposed beaches—use flagged swimming zones only.
Intercity bus frequencies are limited at night; pre-book onward legs in peak holiday weeks.
Windy spells can make seafront activities uncomfortable, especially July-August afternoons.

sweet: Oct-Apr for mild temperatures and lower heat stress than inland Morocco.
